Sports season

| 1996–97 Deutsche Eishockey Liga season | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| League | [Deutsche Eishockey Liga](/wiki/Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga "Deutsche Eishockey Liga") |
| Sport | [Ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") |
| Teams | 16 |
| 1996–97 | |
| Season champions | [Adler Mannheim](/wiki/Adler_Mannheim "Adler Mannheim") |
| [DEL seasons](/wiki/Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga#DEL_seasons "Deutsche Eishockey Liga") | |
| [← 1995–96](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga_season "1995–96 Deutsche Eishockey Liga season")  [1997–98 →](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga_season "1997–98 Deutsche Eishockey Liga season") | |

The **1996–97 Deutsche Eishockey Liga season** was the 3rd season of the [Deutsche Eishockey Liga](/wiki/Deutsche_Eishockey_Liga "Deutsche Eishockey Liga") (English: German Ice Hockey League).

[Adler Mannheim](/wiki/Adler_Mannheim "Adler Mannheim") became the DEL Champion, winning a German title for the second time in their history. Both the [Ratinger Löwen](/wiki/Ratinger_L%C3%B6wen "Ratinger Löwen") and [Wedemark Scorpions](/wiki/Wedemark_Scorpions "Wedemark Scorpions") earned their keep in the league playing against [1st League](/wiki/2nd_Bundesliga_(ice_hockey) "2nd Bundesliga (ice hockey)") EHC Neuwied and TSV Erding.

The [Bosman ruling](/wiki/Bosman_ruling "Bosman ruling"), a 1995 decision of the [European Court of Justice](/wiki/European_Court_of_Justice "European Court of Justice") regarding the movement of labor in [soccer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), had profound influence on the league. The old Bundesliga had national character: German clubs competing for the German title with mostly German players. After the ruling [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") players were excluded from the "foreign" player quota. Within a year, the DEL teams employed 97 EU players and lowered their costs significantly, enabling the smaller teams to compete more effectively. The frequent player moves were not viewed positively by the fans.

Another visible change for the fans was the change in corporate sponsorship. The [Krombacher Brewery](/wiki/Krombacher_Brauerei "Krombacher Brauerei") ended their agreement with the league, resulting in a change in the league logo design. The league would not have a corporate sponsor again until 2003, when the German Yellow Pages ([German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Gelbe Seiten*) signed two consecutive 3-year agreements.

## Regular season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Regular season")]

The mode of play changed for this season. In the first round, all teams played one home and one away game with each other for a total of 30 rounds. The first 6 placed teams continued playing for the playoff placements *Meisterrunde*), and the teams 7-16 played to against relegation/playdowns (*Relegationsrunde*), with only the 2 best-placed teams in this group being eligible for playoffs.

## Playdowns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Playdowns")]

### First round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: First round")]

The first round was played in the [best-of-seven](/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-seven_playoff "Playoff format") mode.

|  |  |  | Game | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Düsseldorfer EG** | - | Ratinger Löwen | **4:0** | 5:1 | 5:3 | 2:0 | 6:2 | – | – | – |
| **Schwenninger Wild Wings** | - | Nürnberg Ice Tigers | **4:1** | 4:1 | 2:3 | 6:1 | 5:3 | 11:3 | – | – |
| **Augsburger Panther** | - | Wedemark Scorpions | **4:0** | 7:1 | 2:1 | 5:4 | 5:3 | – | – | – |
| Frankfurt Lions | - | **Kaufbeurer Adler** | **2:4** | 3:2 | 3:6 | 4:3 | 3:6 | 2:5 | 1:4 | – |

### Second round

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Second round")]

The second round was played in a [best-of-five](/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-five_playoff "Playoff format") mode.

|  |  |  | Game | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Wedemark Scorpions | - | **Nürnberg Ice Tigers** | **1:3** | 4:6 | 4:6 | 5:2 | 2:5 | – |
| **Frankfurt Lions** | - | Ratinger Löwen | **3:0** | 6:1 | 5:4 | 5:3 | – | – |

Wedemark Scorpions (now known as Hannover Scorpions) and Ratinger Löwen (English: Ratinger Lions) had to play against the first two placed teams of the second league.

### Relegation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Relegation")]

The relegation round was played in a [best-of-three](/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-three_playoff "Playoff format") mode.

|  |  |  | Game | 1 | 2 | 3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| EHC Neuwied | - | **Ratinger Löwen** | **0:2** | 2:4 | 3:10 | – |
| **Wedemark Scorpions** | - | TSV Erding | **2:0** | 10:2 | 9:5 | – |

Both the [Ratinger Löwen](/wiki/Ratinger_L%C3%B6wen "Ratinger Löwen") and [Wedemark Scorpions](/wiki/Wedemark_Scorpions "Wedemark Scorpions") earned their keep in the DEL.

## Playoffs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Playoffs")]

All playoff rounds were played in as a [best-of-five](/wiki/Playoff_format#Best-of-five_playoff "Playoff format") series, with the better placed team playing Home-Away-Home-Away-Home.

### Quarterfinals

### Finals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1996%E2%80%9397_DEL_season&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Finals")]

|  |  |  | Game | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Adler Mannheim** | - | Kassel Huskies | **3:0** | 5:4 | 5:2 | 4:2 | – | – |

With the last game, the [Adler Mannheim](/wiki/Adler_Mannheim "Adler Mannheim") became first time DEL Champion and won the German Champion title for the 2nd 8th time in the club history.
